title: Capped snapshot warning appears on bounded bridge-volume ranges
reasoning: The card defaults to the bounded 30d range and range-filters the plotted series, but it forwards the raw dataset-level `isCapped` flag into the chart warning for every range. When Hasura's 1000-row cap is hit, the all-history dataset is partial, but the bounded 7d/30d views are the least likely windows to be truncated. This makes the default chart show a partial-data warning even while displaying a bounded window, creating a false degraded-state signal.
reproduction: Render BridgeVolumeChart with `isCapped={true}`, `hasError={false}`, and the default range. The component passes `hasSnapshotError={true}` to TimeSeriesChartCard before the user selects the all-time range.
recommendation: Gate the cap warning by the active range, e.g. derive `const showCapNote = isCapped && range === "all"` and pass that as `hasSnapshotError`. Keep the chart data filtering unchanged.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included linked tests exercise address-label, auth, breach-history, and breaker components, not BridgeVolumeChart's cap/range behavior. They do not render `BridgeVolumeChart` with `isCapped=true` or assert the warning visibility across range changes.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a BridgeVolumeChart test that renders with `isCapped={true}` on the default 30d range and asserts the partial-data note is absent, then switches to the All range and asserts the note appears.
minimumFixScope: ui-dashboard/src/components/bridge-volume-chart.tsx plus a focused component regression test for cap-note visibility.

history.note: BridgeVolumeChart cap warning is gated to the All range, with default-range hidden and All-range visible regression.
